summary: High‑quality Charlie Miller sound captures a strong LA set with standout Give It A Whirl/Forever Grateful/Forever Dead sequence, though Dire Wolf ends in feedback and Foolish Heart falls flat.
review: The recording benefits from excellent Charlie Miller tape work – a clear soundboard source that only suffers a brief burst of feedback near the end of Dire Wolf. Musically the show shines with an exciting Give It A Whirl > Forever Grateful > Forever Dead medley and a joyful Touch of Grey, while Foolish Heart is noted as a lyrical stumble for Jerry.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (4‑5) and enthusiastic comments about the performance outweigh minor recording hiccups, yielding a strong weighted score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great Charlie Miller tape quality with clear mix; occasional loud feedback burst near end of Dire Wolf that can be jarring on headphones.

standout_songs: Give It A Whirl > Forever Grateful > Forever Dead
show_quality.setlist_flow: The three‑night LA run is described as building momentum, with a particularly strong second half highlighted by the medley and upbeat numbers; overall pacing praised despite one lyrical slip.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Struggles with lyrics on Foolish Heart, forgetting many words.
band_member_comments.Drums: Guest Japanese drummer Fujimoto Yoshikazu appears in the lineup.
